45p/mile, soon to be 34p/mile
Reimbursed public transport

Flat daily £3.50 reduction. Only counts if on a designated "commuting" placement.
schools.leave_details: Need to meet 90% attendance per block, requests are case-by-case but they're usually pretty lenient.

schools.travel_details: Coach
schools.leave_details: 2 days per rotation
schools.scrubs_details: 3 pairs

schools.travel_details: Each trust pays the students individually at the end or after each placement
schools.hardship_funding: hardship fund (separate funds for Home and International students)

schools.travel_details: The University Health Centre (UHC) at Queen's offers student-focused NHS services and University funded non-NHS services for Queen's students. The UHC has extensive experience in the health needs of young adults and are a friendly team who understand University life. International students resident in the UK for six months or more on a student visa are entitled to free NHS care and can also register with the practice
schools.leave_details: Students are offered an allowance of 2 days of discretionary leave in each academic year. This entitles the student to nominate 2 days of leave without providing supporting evidence. And are allowed to take planned absence for these reasons: Please indicate below the category of your leave request   Discretionary / Flexible Leave (maximum of 2 days per academic year)  Education Event (e.g. Presenting a Paper at a Conference)  QUB Ambassadorial Role  Sports (Elite Athlete/ QUB Sports Ambassadorial)  Wedding (Bridal Party Members Only)  Religious Ceremony, where their attendance shouldn't be lower than 75%
schools.hardship_funding: student support fund for Home students and separate university financial assistance fund for ROI/international

schools.travel_details: A fixed amount given to all students depending on their year of study and number of weeks of placement in that year of study. Given in December of academic year (eg this year, Year 5 students receive £689 for the whole year in one lump sum)
schools.leave_details: 80% attendance requirement but approval for leave within the requirement is quite easy. Problem comes when you have missed the requirement and there is a push from them to get students to resit the year rather than do catch up placement
schools.scrubs_details: Each student in the clinical years is provided with black "University of Birmingham Medical Student" embroidered scrubs (2 tops and 2 bottoms) which are mandatory to wear on hospital placement. Students are able to specify their size in a bulk order at the start of the year. Additional scrubs can be purchased - the university provides a link to the supplier website. A student group also organises a "scrubs swap" where final year medical students can donate their old scrubs before they graduate, for clinical students to then buy at a reduced rate.
schools.hardship_funding: student support fund up to £2000 (rarely awarded to International students)

schools.travel_details: Students fill out a form and send it to the school office who process and reimburse a few weeks later. Only applicable to regional students who have to travel and have no accommodation.
schools.leave_details: Varies heavily on a case by case basis. Some students can be given a week off for a conference whilst others just given a day.
schools.scrubs_details: 2x Grey scrubs that aren't the most aesthetically pleasing
schools.passmed_details: Only free passmed for final year and 4th year students
schools.hardship_funding: Brighton bursary up to £500/year not in NHS bursar years (Home students only, no requirement for hardship)

schools.travel_details:
25p per mile or public transport for:
Travel between placement accommodation and GP
Travel to/from community based teaching
Travel back to Bristol from outplacement (only 3 return journeys per year)
Travel back to Bristol for compulsory assessment
Travel for specialist health appointments
schools.leave_details: 4 flexible annual leave days in 4th year, 5 flexible annual leave days in 5th year.
schools.scrubs_details: 1 free set of scrubs per year
schools.hardship_funding: financial assistance fund up to £900

schools.travel_details: Our university do not provide any provision at the moment for additional travel to placement that students may have to undergo. Students are placed at their base hospital for a year during placement, and are expected to travel to GP placement partners in Y1 and Y2 funded by themselves, regardless of how far the GP placement may be from the campus. If students are placed
schools.leave_details: 10 days off (1 day off is counted as a 9-5 day of hospital placement missed)
schools.scrubs_details: 2 pairs of scrubs are provided to each student, BMS branded grey scrubs which specify role (medical student)
schools.hardship_funding: hardship fund

schools.travel_details:
The University of Buckingham reimburses travel for placement under the following rules:

Claims must be submitted within one month of travel.

Receipts must be provided for all public transport costs.

Private car mileage is reimbursed at £0.45 per mile, but only for mileage beyond the first 25 miles in each direction.

Public transport reimbursement only applies to costs above £11.25 per journey each way, representing the equivalent cost of the first 25 miles by car.

No reimbursement is available for any journey under 25 miles each way, regardless of transport method.

Because most Phase 1 placement sites are within 25 miles of the University, students receive no reimbursement for the majority of compulsory placement travel. Many of these sites are difficult to access via direct public transport, often requiring lengthy journeys or the use of taxis for early placements. Over the first two years, this typically results in students personally spending £300 or more on travel to placements. Some taxi journeys to placements can cost as much as 50 pounds one way and if not all members of the group are attending the placement the cost of taxis can be very expensive for a few students to share. The same reimbursement system applies for Phase 2 students (Year 3 and 4 and final year). However, students usually only have to travel for GP blocks and Mental Health blocks and the same 25 mile requirement applies.
schools.leave_details: The university provides days off for events and conferences and these requests have to be submitted 2 weeks in advance of the date. The university does not specify how many days off are provided for such events. However, if the total number of absences (including unauthorised absences and authorised absences such as for conferences or personal circumstances) exceeds 15 days per academic year, the student will receive a Level 1 Professionalism Concern.
schools.hardship_funding: hardship fund up to £500

schools.travel_details: for MB/PhD reimbursed at 45p per mile.
schools.hardship_funding: bursary up to £3500/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship) but variable (but in some cases very generous) support from Colleges

schools.travel_details: There’s a claim system on Learning central where you fill out a form with the relevant details and email this to them with receipts and they will send the money to you within a week
schools.leave_details: We are allowed to have 3 mental health days per year but they can’t be back to back. Additionally, sick leave can be up to a week without any GP/hospital evidence needed. If you have a counselling session you are allowed to miss teaching and missing days for conferences will not get you penalised
schools.passmed_details: If you are pasty if MedSoc you get a free pastest subscription and the uni give you a free complete Anatomy subscription
schools.hardship_funding: Cardiff - bursary £500/year not in NHS bursary years AND NEED TO CHECK IF AVAILABLE IN OTHER YEARS AS WEBSITE ISN‘T CLEAR (students receiving UK loans only, no requirement for hardship) + money support fund for hardship; Bangor - bursary up to £1000 (Home students only, no requirement for hardship)
student support fund

schools.travel_details:
We get ferry’s reimbursed for rural placement and for a 4 week placement we get a return trip reimbursed and for 8 weeks we get 2 returns reimbursed. To follow is a quote from an updated travel email. ‘To limit the use of Medical ACT funding for travel and subsistence to support placements at least 30 minutes away from the Medical School by public transport AND more than 20 miles by car.
Where bus travel is possible and the student is under 22, it is expected that the student should make use of the Young Persons’ (under 22) Free Bus Travel Scheme, or any equivalent scheme.
Mileage allowance for car travel which can be claimed to Medical ACT will be at the Public Transport rate of 24p per mile in keeping with the NES Travel Policy and is intended to contribute to fuel costs.
Additional passengers - where the claimant has carried a passenger attending the same placement, the claimant may claim a passenger mileage rate of 5p/mile.
The most cost-effective form of transport should be used at all times.’
schools.scrubs_details: 2 tops and 2 bottoms that are embroidered University of Dundee Medical Student
schools.hardship_funding: funding support (Home students only)

schools.travel_details:
Bursary given for each cohort
Foundation Year - £75
Y1 - £135
Y2 - £210
Y3 - £750 + £60 for students at MWL trust for parking
Y4 - £1000 + £35 for MWL students’ parking
Y5 - £700 + parking for MWL students
schools.leave_details: 4 days - can take these days off from a range of ‘personal reasons’ if you need a day off to appointments or extracurricular activities

schools.travel_details: Exeter have a really good travel policy. Any pre-organised travel (e.g. to GP or different trusts) is paid at the beginning of term, along with an £80 one off payment at the start of the year for other costs which come with travel. Any unexpected expenses can be claimed using the travel claim form and is paid at the end of term. Students generally feel well supported by our travel policy.
schools.scrubs_details: In year 1 we are given 2 pairs of scrubs, plain scrubs with no medical school logo. These are supposed to last us the whole 5/6 years, clinical years are on placement 4 days a week so most people have to externally buy extra pairs.
schools.hardship_funding: Success for All fund (for hardship, rarely awarded to International students)

schools.travel_details: Health board dependent
schools.leave_details: Variable to the situation. Days off have been provided for personal circumstances and conference attendance
schools.hardship_funding: Discretionary fund for Home students and separate financial support fund for International students

schools.travel_details: We typically receive advance payment from the medical school for our GP travel requirements and any other clinics we may need to travel for (though this is rare). We rarely have a claim system for example after the Year 4 conference, the medical school allowed students not based in York (where the conference was held) to claim transport. For exams such as OSCEs, there is often a coach put on for students. For example the Year 2 OSCE is held in Hull, there was a coach for York based students. This applies less in clinical years when the medical school expects us to make our own way
schools.leave_details: Absences classed as planned (e.g. conference attendance) or unplanned (e.g. illness). Total of 6 missed sessions triggers an email to the student from the SLO (student liasion) team. If 10 sessions are missed, the Phase Lead will email student with a letter and request further information. If a student’s cumulative absence is more than 2 weeks (20 sessions) from any one block, rotation or attachment then the Phase Lead will meet the student, and a leave of absence will be discussed. The student would also be reported to Case Management Group. If the student is absent cumulatively 4 weeks (40 sessions) across the year, then a leave of absence will be required
schools.scrubs_details: 2 free scrub tops and trousers before the beginning of Year 3
schools.other_resources_details: Complete Anatomy subscription for Year 1 and 2 students
schools.hardship_funding: Hull York medical school bursary up to £1000/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship)

schools.travel_details:

Bus to placements further afield once every morning and evening
If you drive or cycle, claim mileage but you must take the shortest route
schools.leave_details: Phase directors/heads of year have discretion to approve short periods of emergency leave from programme on compassionate grounds, permitted leave for religious practices, students may request leave for up to 3 days per academic year to attend conferences or other extracurricular events. All absences submitted at least 3 weeks in advance for review by relevant phase director/head of year. Students will be notified of the response

schools.travel_details:
There is a set amount of money which is the remaining budget from the rest of the year. Every mile every student does accumulates a total number of miles which is then used to calculate the pence per mile at which each student will be paid. 
I had a meeting with the powers-that-be and they said that they've run out of money and so won't even be doing that after the end of next academic year.
Every student is entitled to the money regardless of whether they were a passenger or driver.
Lots of flaws in the system but given that it's getting rid of entirely there isn't a huge amount I can do.
schools.leave_details: 5 days, we have exclusion periods that we cannot use it in and it cannot be used for more than 3 days at a time.

schools.travel_details:
Travel reimbursement is based on Additional Costs of Journeys (ACJs) which is how much
extra it costs students to go to placement instead of the normal place of study. ACJs are not
the entire journey cost
2. To claim travel reimbursement, the cost of travel to and from the placement must be more
than the normal daily travel cost to and from Guy’s Campus and/or a central Hub site.
However, if accomodation is offered on peripheral placements, travel is NOT reimbursed more than 1 journey an 8 week block

schools.travel_details:
Some year groups get advance payment while others have the claim back system. For example, in third year, students get an advance payment of roughly £700 (there is a fixed reimbursement pot, and based on how many students claim they require reimbursement, the pot is split evenly per student). This is irrespective of placement site (aka near vs long distance sites).
This is not the case in Year 4, as some students receive the NHS bursary while others do no. Therefore the SOM has a claim back system, where students show their receipts and mileage to claim. Students on the NHS bursary do not receive support from the SOM (school of medicine).
This is the same in Year 5.
schools.leave_details: The minimum attendance for Liverpool (80% of clinical placement) which calculates to about 2 full days of placement each month. If attendance is less than 80%, you will have to resit the rotation over summer. More than 2 resits required means that you resit the year. Sick days are often approved without evidence needed, but conferences and events need to evidence to be approved. AT most, the university is happy to give half a day off (unless the conference requires travelling).
schools.scrubs_details: You get one pair of scrubs in first year, 4 more pairs in second year. We also have a recycling system where 5th years can donate their scrubs to the school for other students to collect for free.
schools.passmed_details: Other subscriptions such as Capsule, the PSA exam portal (if that counts) and Red Whale for Gp content
schools.hardship_funding: Liverpool bursary up to £2000/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship) + hardship fund

schools.travel_details:
Students in T, P, and F years are eligible to claim travel expenses for their clinical placements, and, in some cases, accommodation expenses too. Ultimately, this looks at the different between your usual cost to travel to university, and the cost to travel to your placement site. 

I.e. if a student lives in zone 1 in London, and travels to St George's in zone 3 for university, that would be their usual cost of travel. If they were to then have a placement in zone 5, they would be eligible to claim back the difference in cost of travel to and from placement. I.e. Zone 5 travel cost daily - Zone 3 travel cost daily. 

There are additional parts to this, such as for taxis, and accommodation listed below. 

Cycling is reimbursed at 30p per mile 
driving is reimbursed at 42p per mile 
Passengers in cars are reimbursed at 7.5p per mile

There are no travel costs reimbursed for years 1 and 2. The above applies only to years 3-5. However in pre clinical years, certainly when I was a student at that stage, £150 across two Oyster cards was provided, so cover the cost of GP placements. 



Taxis; 
Students are expected to travel via the cheapest method of transport. Taxi use is not permitted and will only be considered in exceptional one-off emergency circumstances and with prior consultation with the university. 
Generally, if approved, claims for taxi travel will usually be reimbursed at the equivalent public transport rate or mileage rate set by NHS BSA (i.e. 42p per mile), however, all requests will be reviewed and considered case by case. For NHS claims, any taxi reimbursement is at the discretion of NHS.
In any case, costs that are supposed to be covered by the DSA travel allowance cannot be reimbursed via travel claims.
Parking
Parking costs can usually be reimbursed with sufficient evidence. Please note, it may not always be possible to park at the hospital site and you may need to find alternative parking yourself. Please keep in mind that students are expected to travel using the cheapest method of transport, so please factor parking costs into this.
Details on parking at some hospitals can be found on the following list: Hospital Car Parking Information This is subject to change so in all cases, please check with the UG Coordinator at the relevant Trust to discuss parking spaces/permits.

Year 4/5
Students in receipt of an NHS Student Bursary may be eligible to have their travel and accommodation costs refunded by the NHS BSA. 

If you are not eligible to claim via the NHS BSA, you can claim for excess travel costs only from City St George’s using this form: T, P and F Year City St George's Travel Claim Form

In line with City St George’s Expenses Policy, claims must be submitted within three months of the final day of the placement for which you are making a claim.
It will usually take around 2-3 weeks for payments to be issued once the claim is processed and submitted to Finance. During peak times this may take a little longer.
schools.leave_details: 3 days for conferences, 1 per block of placement for personal health/study days.
schools.hardship_funding: Opportunity fund grant up to £1700/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship) + access to learning fund for Home students and separate fund for International students Tooting campus - check medical student availability

schools.travel_details:
Phase 1 - travel support is covered by the University's scholarship and bursary offer.  
link: https://www.sunderland.ac.uk/terms-and-conditions/scholarships-and-bursaries/ 


Phase 2: travel and accommodation is supported by Undergraduate Medical Tarriff, which is sent directly to placement providers. 

Travel and accommodation rates are in line with NHSBA TDAE rates. 

- support is provided for return travel b/w base site and university (SPINE days - which are Fridays)
- from base site to MH placement
- and from base site to GP placement

Also, our formative/Summative OSCEs will take place at different base sites so travel support for return journeys from base site to site of OSCE will be provided after allocations have been made.

Payment is made directly into student account by the University.

We will receive half of LEP travel support payment towards the end of Semester 1 and the remainder within semester 2. 

For accommodation expenses - you book and pay for it and then send the receipt to Medical school placement team for reimbursement. 

The further your LEP base site is, the more you are reimbursed for travel. 

For GP placement, it depends on the distance of your GP (in miles) from your LEP. 

For 4th year - the LEP travel support you would get is less than what you get in 4th year. e.g. 
4th year - North Tees & Hartlepool NHS Foundation Trust - £539.35
3rd year - North Tees and Hartlepool NHS Foundation Trust £887.71

Phase 3 (year 5) travel and accommodation for students eligible for the NHS Bursary is via the Travel & Dual Accommodation Expenses (TDAE). Those who are not eligible for NHS bursary can still claim TDAE offer from the university.
schools.leave_details: Absences can be unplanned (illness/bereavement...), planned (doctor appt...), and flexible leave (6 half days or 3 full days - request form must be submitted atleast 1 week before). flexible cannot be taken where attendance is mandatory e.g. exams, induction etc...
schools.scrubs_details: Every year students are given one set of scrubs, for first 3 years of Med school. In the first year we are given a lab coat for lab practicals and anatomy sessions.
schools.hardship_funding: Student support fund (Home students only?); bursary criteria include location of residence
Hardship find; bursary criteria include location of residence

schools.travel_details:
Follows NHS bursary guidelines. If not receiving bursary there are no travel specific bursaries for students.
Taxis can be provided for students between placement sites if arranged the day before or morning of

schools.scrubs_details: 3. “hand me downs” available for free from edu centre
schools.hardship_funding: Hardship fund (Home students only)

schools.travel_details: Reimbursement for travel that meets certain criteria although it is poorly advertised
schools.scrubs_details: 1 pair of unisex Uneek Scrubs
schools.hardship_funding: Bursary up to £3000/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship) + hardship fund

schools.travel_details:
fuel cost - 42p 
Taxi -Taxi reimbursement will be up to the cost of public transport. Journeys to locations inaccessible via public transport; over 90 minutes from Preston bus/train station.
schools.leave_details: negotiable leave form needs to be competled and approved by the team, allow to have 2 weeks off - any missed placement requires catch up.
schools.scrubs_details: 2 pairs of scrubs given at the start of year 1. 2 pairs of pants (blue) 2 pairs of tops (brown
schools.passmed_details: geeky medic only the access to the question bank , only given only in year 3 and 4.
schools.hardship_funding: students in hardship advised to email

schools.travel_details:
Years 1-4: Transport provided in the form of taxi or chartered minibus/coach, with a few exceptions, in which case mileage will be reimbursed at 45ppm, or off-peak public transport can be expensed. Students have to notify the placement team beforehand how they intend to travel, and will only be reimbursed if they fulfilled this (presumably to avoid passengers in carpools trying to claim for public transport they didn't take etc).

Year 5: Set allowances proportionate to the module. E.g. a 28 day placement at James Paget University Hospital, 26 miles away from UEA, has an allowance of £660.96 to pay for 24 commuting days, but an 8 week placement with 40 commuting days has a £1,101.60 allowance.
schools.leave_details: I cannot find any published limit on the number of days off. We must provide minimum 10 days notice and it is at the discretion of the Year Lead (to my knowledge). Categories on the form are award ceremony, conference, court attendance, driving test, family event, operation, paternity leave, religious events, visa issues, or other.
schools.scrubs_details: Basic dencowear Scrubs, 4 tops 2 trousers
schools.hardship_funding: Bursary up to £1600/year (Home students only, no requirement for hardship) + student services fund (Home students and those with visa access to public funds only)

schools.travel_details: There are two windows, September and January to put in a form and claim a one off payment of 6.25 per day for travel if your placement is >30miles away
schools.hardship_funding: 10% off student fee (NI domiciled students, no requirement for hardship) + financial hardship fund (Home students only)

schools.travel_details: NHS bursary
schools.leave_details: Warwick has a set number of annual leave days to be used as you prefer. These are automatically granted if given with over 6 weeks notice, and still considered if given with less time. They also grant extra leave for educational experiences such as conferences
schools.scrubs_details: 2 sets of blue WMS branded scrubs
schools.hardship_funding: Warwick bursary up to £2500/year (Home students execpt Wales only, no requirement for hardship) + hardship fund for Home students and separate fund for international students
